text.title: Marketing Tool Stack Rationalization Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Marketing Tool Stack Rationalization MUST enforce. Rationale + source per rule. Rules referenced by id from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Inventory MUST list every tool (login URL, monthly cost, last-used date, named owner, workflow-tier). Hidden tools = audit invalid.
rule.rationale: Most rationalization fails because the visible inventory is 60-70% complete; shadow-IT contains the worst offenders.

rule.statement: Every tool MUST have a single named owner. Tools without owner are auto-categorized Kill.
rule.rationale: Un-owned tools have no advocate; they survive on inertia, not value.

rule.statement: Usage score MUST cite evidence (last-login timestamp, MAU, billable seats actually used). Self-reported usage is rejected.
rule.rationale: Teams over-report tool value by 30-50%; only audit-log evidence is admissible.

rule.statement: Two tools serving the same workflow tier MUST be consolidated unless lock-in cost > 12 months of duplicate licensing.
rule.rationale: Duplicate-workflow tooling is the cheapest savings; only deep lock-in justifies keeping both.

rule.statement: Report MUST ship with a 30-day execution checklist (Kill before renewal / Consolidate by date / Owner assigned). Reports without execution plan are not done.
rule.rationale: Audits without action plans yield 5-10% of available savings; execution plans yield 60-80%.

rule.statement: If applicability preconditions are unmet (see AGENTS.md Skip If), the methodology MUST be skipped and a one-line skip-reason logged.
rule.rationale: A named skip terminal keeps decision-tree leaves resolvable and prevents silent misuse on misfit inputs.
